Class HiddenChainAttackBehavior

java.lang.Object
dom.institution.lab.cns.bitcoin.node.DefaultNodeBehavior
dom.institution.lab.cns.bitcoin.node.HiddenChainAttackBehavior
All Implemented Interfaces:
NodeBehaviorStrategy
public class HiddenChainAttackBehavior extends DefaultNodeBehavior
Implements a hidden chain attack strategy for Bitcoin network simulation.

A HiddenChainAttackBehavior wraps the honest node behavior and allows an attacker to secretly mine on a private chain while potentially appearing honest to the network. The attacker can then strategically release the hidden chain when advantageous (e.g., to double-spend transactions or reorganize the blockchain).

This class manages three distinct operational states:

  • HiddenChainAttackBehavior.State.IDLE: Normal honest operation, no attack in progress.
  • HiddenChainAttackBehavior.State.MONITORING: Honest operation while observing network for attack opportunities.
  • HiddenChainAttackBehavior.State.ATTACKING: Private chain mining in progress, blocks not yet released to network.

Attack initiation and chain release are controlled by configurable parameters: attackPower (mining capability in trials per time unit), targetTransaction (transaction ID to trigger attack onset), startAdvantage (height disadvantage threshold that triggers attack), and releaseAdvantage (height advantage threshold that triggers chain release).

See Also:
  • HonestNodeBehavior
  • BitcoinNode
  • NodeBehaviorStrategy

  • Constructor Details

    • HiddenChainAttackBehavior

      public HiddenChainAttackBehavior(BitcoinNode node, HonestNodeBehavior beh, HonestNodeBehavior altBeh)
      Constructs a hidden chain attack behavior and binds it to a specific BitcoinNode instance.

      Initializes the behavior in IDLE state with a wrapped HonestNodeBehavior and empty hidden chain. Attack parameters (startAdvantage, releaseAdvantage, attackPower) must be configured via setters before entering MONITORING state.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires node != null;
   //@ requires beh != null;
   //@ requires altBeh != null;
   //@ ensures this.node == node;
   //@ ensures currentState == State.IDLE;
   //@ ensures hiddenChain != null && hiddenChain.isEmpty();
   //@ ensures honestBehavior != null;
      Parameters:
      node - the Bitcoin node to which this behavior is attached (must not be null)
      beh - the honest behavior to wrap and delegate to (must not be null)
      altBeh - a limited version of honest behavior to wrap and delegate to (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if node is null

  • Method Details

    • event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction

      public void event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t, long time)
      Handles transactions received directly from clients.

      Behavior depends on current attack state:

      • IDLE: Delegates to honest behavior; normal operation.
      • MONITORING: Accepts all transactions except the target transaction (target must arrive in a block to trigger attack).
      • ATTACKING: Rejects the target transaction (should not arrive directly); accepts others to maintain honest appearance.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires t != null;
   //@ requires time >= 0;
   //@ requires currentState != null;
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesClientTransaction in class DefaultNodeBehavior
      Parameters:
      t - the received transaction (must not be null)
      time - the current simulation time (must be non-negative)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if t is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if time is negative
      IllegalStateException - if target transaction arrives during ATTACKING state

    • event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction

      public void event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction t, long time)
      Handles transactions propagated from other nodes in the network.

      Behavior depends on current attack state:

      • IDLE: Delegates to honest behavior; normal propagation handling.
      • MONITORING: Ignores target transaction; forwards others normally.
      • ATTACKING: Maintains transaction pool on public chain while privately mining hidden chain; accepts all propagated transactions.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires t != null;
   //@ requires time >= 0;
   //@ requires currentState != null;
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesPropagatedTransaction in class DefaultNodeBehavior
      Parameters:
      t - the propagated transaction (must not be null)
      time - the current simulation time (must be non-negative)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if t is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if time is negative

    • event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer

      public void event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.ITxContainer c)
      Handles reception of a propagated block from the network.

      Behavior depends on current attack state:

      • IDLE: Delegates to honest behavior; normal block handling.
      • MONITORING: Delegates to honest behavior; if the block contains the target transaction, sets the hidden chain tip to the block's parent and considers initiating the attack via considerAttacking().
      • ATTACKING: Delegates to honest behavior to update the public chain; advantage is re-evaluated in event_NodeCompletesValidation(ITxContainer, long).

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires c != null;
   //@ requires c instanceof Block;
   //@ requires currentState != null;
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Specified by:
      event_NodeReceivesPropagatedContainer in class DefaultNodeBehavior
      Parameters:
      c - the propagated block container (must not be null, must be a Block)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if c is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if c is not a Block instance

    • event_NodeCompletesValidation

      public void event_NodeCompletesValidation(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.ITxContainer c, long time)
      Handles completion of block validation by this node.

      Behavior depends on attack state:

      • IDLE: Delegates to honest behavior; block added to public blockchain.
      • MONITORING: Delegates to honest behavior; block added to public blockchain.
      • ATTACKING: Block is intercepted and added to the hidden chain instead of the public blockchain. After addition, evaluates whether the release advantage threshold has been reached.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires c != null;
   //@ requires c instanceof Block;
   //@ requires time >= 0;
   //@ requires currentState != null;
      Specified by:
      event_NodeCompletesValidation in interface NodeBehaviorStrategy
      Specified by:
      event_NodeCompletesValidation in class DefaultNodeBehavior
      Parameters:
      c - the validated block (must not be null, must be a Block)
      time - the current simulation time (must be non-negative)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if c is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if c is not a Block, or time is negative

    • isWorthMining

      protected boolean isWorthMining()
      Description copied from class: DefaultNodeBehavior
      Determines whether the current mining pool is sufficiently valuable to justify mining activity.
      Overrides:
      isWorthMining in class DefaultNodeBehavior
      Returns:
      true if the total value of the node’s mining pool exceeds BitcoinNode.getMinValueToMine(), otherwise false

    • goToMonitoringState

      public void goToMonitoringState()
      Transitions the attack state from IDLE to MONITORING.

      Enables passive observation of the network for attack opportunities. In MONITORING state, the node operates honestly while waiting for a block containing the target transaction, which automatically triggers transition to ATTACKING state. No blocks are mined secretly in MONITORING state.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires currentState == State.IDLE;
   //@ ensures currentState == State.MONITORING;
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if not currently in IDLE state

    • goToIdleState

      public void goToIdleState()
      Transitions the attack state from MONITORING back to IDLE.

      Stops passive observation and returns to normal honest operation. No parameters are reset; the node can re-enter MONITORING with the same configuration.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires currentState == State.MONITORING;
   //@ ensures currentState == State.IDLE;
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if not currently in MONITORING state

    • cancelAttack

      public void cancelAttack()
      Cancels an ongoing attack without releasing the hidden chain.

      Discards all accumulated hidden blocks and returns to IDLE state without broadcasting anything to the network. Called when attack conditions are no longer favorable (e.g., the public chain has outpaced the hidden chain).

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires currentState == State.ATTACKING;
   //@ ensures currentState == State.IDLE;
   //@ ensures hiddenChain.isEmpty();
   //@ ensures hiddenChainTip == null;

    • processPostValidationActivities

      protected void processPostValidationActivities(long time)
      Performs cleanup and re-initialization steps following hidden block validation.

      Stops mining, resets the next validation event, removes validated transactions from the mining pool, reconstructs the pool, and reconsiders whether to continue mining the next hidden block.

      Overrides:
      processPostValidationActivities in class DefaultNodeBehavior
      Parameters:
      time - the current simulation time

    • completeAttack

      public void completeAttack(long time)
      Releases the accumulated hidden chain to the network.

      Transitions to IDLE state, then broadcasts each block in the hidden chain to the network (both receiving it locally and cloning it for propagation).

      The released chain may reorganize the public blockchain if it is longer than the current main chain.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires currentState == State.ATTACKING;
   //@ requires !hiddenChain.isEmpty();
   //@ ensures currentState == State.IDLE;
   //@ ensures hiddenChain.isEmpty();
   //@ ensures hiddenChainTip == null;
      Parameters:
      time - the current simulation time for broadcast timestamp
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if not in ATTACKING state

    • inHiddenChain

      public boolean inHiddenChain(dom.institution.lab.cns.engine.transaction.Transaction tx)
      Determines whether a given transaction is contained in any block of the hidden chain.

      Traverses the hidden chain from tip to root, checking each block for the transaction. Returns false immediately if no attack is in progress (i.e., the hidden chain tip is null). This method is state-independent: it may be called in any attack state, but will only return true while an attack is active and the transaction has been mined into a hidden block.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires tx != null;
   //@ ensures hiddenChainTip == null ==> \result == false;
   //@ ensures (\exists Block b; hiddenChain.contains(b); b.contains(tx))
   //            ==> \result == true;
   //@ ensures (\forall Block b; hiddenChain.contains(b); !b.contains(tx))
   //            ==> \result == false;
      Parameters:
      tx - the transaction to search for (must not be null)
      Returns:
      true if tx is found in any block of the hidden chain; false otherwise, including when no attack is in progress

    • getCurrentConfirmations

      public int getCurrentConfirmations()

    • getReleaseConfirmations

      public Integer getReleaseConfirmations()

    • setReleaseConfirmations

      public void setReleaseConfirmations(Integer releaseConfirmations)

    • getReleaseStrategy

      public HiddenChainAttackBehavior.ReleaseStrategy getReleaseStrategy()

    • getAttackState

      public HiddenChainAttackBehavior.State getAttackState()
      Returns the current operational state of the attack.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result != null;
   //@ ensures \result == currentState;
      Returns:
      the current HiddenChainAttackBehavior.State

    • getCurrentAdvantage

      public int getCurrentAdvantage()
      Returns the current advantage (hidden chain height minus public chain height).

      Positive values indicate the hidden chain is ahead; zero or negative means it is behind. This value determines whether to release the hidden chain.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires node != null;
   //@ requires node.getStructure() != null;
   //@ requires node.getStructure().getLongestTip() != null;
   //@ ensures \result == (hiddenChainTip == null ? 0 : hiddenChainTip.height)
   //                       - longestTip.height;
      Returns:
      the advantage in blocks (can be negative if hidden chain is behind)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if node, blockchain structure, or longest tip is null

    • setAttackPower

      public void setAttackPower(float power)
      Sets the attacker's mining power, i.e. the power that the attacker will assume once the attack starts. The attacker will revert to original power after the attack

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires power > 0;
   //@ ensures this.attackPower == power;
      Parameters:
      power - the mining power in trials per time unit (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if power <= 0

    • getAttackPower

      public float getAttackPower()
      Returns the attacker's mining power.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result == this.attackPower;
      Returns:
      the mining power in trials per time unit

    • setNetworkHonestPower

      public void setNetworkHonestPower(float f)

    • getNetworkHonestPower

      public float getNetworkHonestPower()

    • setTargetTransaction

      public void setTargetTransaction(long txID)
      Sets the target transaction for the attack.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires txID >= -1;
   //@ ensures this.targetTransaction == txID;
      Parameters:
      txID - the transaction ID to target (-1 for no specific target)

    • getTargetTransaction

      public long getTargetTransaction()
      Returns the target transaction ID.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result == this.targetTransaction;
      Returns:
      the target transaction ID (-1 if no target set)

    • setStartAdvantage

      public void setStartAdvantage(Integer startAdv)
      Sets the advantage threshold that triggers attack start.

      Typically zero or negative. For example, -2 means "start when 2 blocks behind". The attack starts when getAdvantage() <= startAdvantage. Must be set before entering MONITORING state.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures this.startAdvantage == startAdv;
      Parameters:
      startAdv - the start advantage threshold (typically zero or negative)

    • getStartAdvantage

      public Integer getStartAdvantage()
      Returns the start advantage threshold.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result == this.startAdvantage;
      Returns:
      the start advantage threshold (null if not set)

    • setReleaseAdvantage

      public void setReleaseAdvantage(Integer relAdv)
      Sets the advantage threshold that triggers hidden chain release.

      Typically zero or positive. For example, 2 means "release when 2 blocks ahead". The chain is released when getAdvantage() >= releaseAdvantage. Zero reproduces Nakamoto attack probabilities. Must be set before entering MONITORING state.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures this.releaseAdvantage == relAdv;
      Parameters:
      relAdv - the release advantage threshold (typically zero or positive)

    • getReleaseAdvantage

      public Integer getReleaseAdvantage()
      Returns the release advantage threshold.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result == this.releaseAdvantage;
      Returns:
      the release advantage threshold (null if not set)

    • getHonestBehavior

      public HonestNodeBehavior getHonestBehavior()
      Returns the wrapped honest behavior strategy.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result == this.honestBehavior;
      Returns:
      the HonestNodeBehavior instance

    • setHonestBehavior

      public void setHonestBehavior(HonestNodeBehavior honestBehavior)
      Sets the wrapped honest behavior strategy.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures this.honestBehavior == honestBehavior;
      Parameters:
      honestBehavior - the honest behavior to wrap (must not be null)

    • setAttackTimeOut

      public void setAttackTimeOut(long timeOut)
      Sets the simulation time at which an ongoing MONITORING or ATTACKING phase must be forcibly terminated.

      When the simulation clock reaches this value,

      invalid reference 
      #handleTimeOut()
      should be called to abort the attack. A value of -1 (or any sentinel agreed by the caller) indicates that no timeout is in effect.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ requires timeOut >= 0;
   //@ ensures this.attackTimeOut == timeOut;
      Parameters:
      timeOut - the simulation time (in milliseconds from sim start) after which the attack must cease; must be non-negative
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if timeOut is negative

    • getAttackTimeOut

      public long getAttackTimeOut()
      Returns the simulation time at which the current MONITORING or ATTACKING phase will be forcibly terminated.

      JML Contract:

      
   //@ ensures \result == this.attackTimeOut;
      Returns:
      the timeout value in simulation-time milliseconds

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the attack behavior state.

      Format:

       HiddenChainAttackBehavior{
     currentState=ATTACKING,
     attackPower=1.5,
     targetTransaction=42,
     startAdvantage=-2,
     releaseAdvantage=2,
     advantage=1,
     hiddenChainLength=5
 }
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      formatted string with attack state, configuration, and current metrics

    • printHiddenChain

      public String printHiddenChain(String sep)
      Returns a comma-separated string of block IDs in the hidden chain, ordered from the chain root to the tip. Returns an empty string if hiddenChainTip is null.
      Returns:
      block IDs from root to tip, e.g. "5,6,7", or "" if empty

    • printHiddenChainAndContent

      public String printHiddenChainAndContent(String sep)
